title: EU MiFID II Cost and Charges Disclosure
content:
This document outlines Meridian Wealth Partners' cost and charges disclosure requirements under MiFID II Article 24(4) and the corresponding ESMA Guidelines on certain aspects of the MiFID II suitability requirements. All client-facing associates must understand these disclosure obligations to ensure full regulatory compliance across our EU operations.

Direct Investment Service Costs: Our advisory fees range from 0.75% to 1.50% annually depending on portfolio size and service level. For portfolios exceeding €2 million, our standard advisory fee is 1.00% per annum, calculated quarterly on the average daily balance. Discretionary portfolio management services carry an additional fee of 0.25% annually. Transaction charges apply at €25 per equity trade for portfolios under €500,000, with reduced rates of €15 per transaction for larger portfolios.

Third-Party Product Costs: When recommending UCITS funds, we must disclose the ongoing charges figure (OCF) which typically ranges from 0.15% for passive index funds to 2.25% for actively managed alternative investment strategies. For our preferred fund platform, the average OCF across equity funds is 1.45%, while fixed income funds average 0.85%. Performance fees, where applicable, typically range from 15% to 20% of returns above the specified benchmark, subject to high-water mark provisions.

Ancillary Service Charges: Custody services are provided through our institutional partners at rates between 0.05% and 0.15% annually depending on asset classes held. Foreign exchange transactions incur spreads of 0.25% to 0.75% depending on currency pair and transaction size. Settlement and clearing fees for international securities average €35 per transaction, while domestic EU settlements typically cost €12 per trade.

Ex-Ante Cost Disclosure Requirements: Under ESMA Guidelines, we must provide clients with aggregated cost disclosures showing the cumulative impact of all charges over 1, 3, 5, and 10-year holding periods. Our standard disclosure template assumes a €100,000 investment with 7% annual gross returns. For a balanced portfolio with 1.25% total annual charges, the projected cumulative cost impact would be €1,250 (year 1), €4,125 (year 3), €7,500 (year 5), and €17,250 (year 10), representing the compound effect of charges on investment growth.

Ex-Post Reporting Obligations: Annual suitability reports must include actual costs incurred, expressed both in absolute terms and as percentages of average portfolio value. Our portfolio management system automatically calculates these figures quarterly, with year-end statements showing total costs paid and performance impact. For 2024, our average client paid 1.35% in total annual charges across all services and underlying investments.

Special Considerations: Complex products including structured notes, alternative investment funds, and derivatives require enhanced cost disclosure. Marketing making spreads on structured products typically range from 1.5% to 4.0% depending on underlying complexity and market conditions. Alternative investment funds may include subscription fees (2-3%), management fees (1.5-2.5%), performance fees (15-25%), and redemption charges (0.5-2.0%).

Compliance Requirements: All cost disclosures must be provided before investment advice is given, updated when material changes occur, and included in annual suitability assessments. Documentation must be maintained for audit purposes and periodic supervisory reviews. Associates should use the standardized disclosure templates available in our compliance portal and escalate any unusual fee structures to the compliance team for review before client presentation.
